message:
refactor(cx): migrate checkpoint timestamps from wall-clock Instant to runtime Time

CheckpointState tracked progress using std::time::Instant, which
means the deadline monitor compared wall-clock durations against
logical runtime deadlines — a category mismatch that caused false
stale-progress warnings in deterministic/lab runtimes where wall
time barely advances while logical time races ahead, and missed
warnings in the inverse scenario.

This commit switches CheckpointState::last_checkpoint from
Option<Instant> to Option<Time> (the runtime's logical clock type):

task_context.rs:
- last_checkpoint field: Instant → Time
- record() / record_at(): accept Time instead of Instant
- record_with_message() / record_with_message_at(): same migration
- Fallback record()/record_with_message() now call crate::time::wall_now()
  instead of Instant::now(), keeping standalone (non-runtime) usage working
- Tests updated to use Time::from_nanos() literals instead of Instant::now()

cx.rs:
- New helper current_checkpoint_time(): reads the timer driver handle
  from Cx's handles (if present) to get the authoritative runtime
  clock, falling back to wall_clock_now() when no driver exists
- checkpoint() and checkpoint_with(): capture current_checkpoint_time()
  before acquiring the inner write lock, then pass it to record_at()
  / record_with_message_at() — this ensures the timestamp is taken at
  the call site rather than after potentially contended lock acquisition
- checkpoint_state() doc example simplified to just check .is_some()
  since the Time type doesn't have an .elapsed() method like Instant

deadline_monitor.rs:
- MonitoredTask: last_progress renamed to last_progress_wall (still
  Instant, tracks wall-clock for checkpoint_timeout comparison);
  last_checkpoint_seen changed from Option<Instant> to Option<Time>;
  new field last_checkpoint_count_seen: u64 for count-based freshness
- New task registration: last_progress_time uses the checkpoint's
  runtime Time (or task.created_at() as fallback) instead of the
  current scan instant
- Existing task update: uses checkpoint_count > entry count (instead
  of checkpoint timestamp ordering) to detect fresh checkpoints,
  avoiding false negatives when two checkpoints occur at the same
  logical time
- checkpoint_interval calculation: uses Time::duration_since (returns
  nanos) wrapped in Duration::from_nanos
- Wall-no-progress check: uses saturating_duration_since to prevent
  panic if the Instant source is non-monotonic (e.g. mocked clocks)
- Scan interval gate: also uses saturating_duration_since for safety
- Tests: all make_task calls updated to pass Option<Time> instead of
  Option<Instant>; removed test_now_minus helper (no longer needed);
  three new tests: repeated_checkpoint_count_resets_progress,
  no_warning_when_checkpoint_time_ahead_of_monitor_clock,
  check_interval_tolerates_time_getter_going_backwards

audit/ambient.rs:
- AMBIENT_VIOLATION_CEILING lowered from 120 to 118, reflecting the
  two removed ambient Cx::current() calls in dns/resolver.rs and
  server/shutdown.rs (committed separately but counted here)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
